summary: The correct answer is B because internal fixation using lag screws provides biomechanical stabilization by distributing loads across fractured bone segments—achieving effective load sharing between adjacent bones instead of transferring concentrated forces that could compromise union. This concept underpins successful treatment outcomes for symphyseal fractures when proper fixation ensures structural integrity while allowing natural biological repair processes.
